content.problemStatement:
When working with flexbox, the container (the element with `display: flex;`) controls the positioning of the items inside it. You use two main properties on the container to arrange the items: `justify-content` and `align-items`.

**justify-content**\
This property controls how items are aligned along the **main axis**. The main axis is the primary direction the items are flowing in (by default, this is left to right).

- `flex-start`: Pushes all the items to the beginning of the container.  
- `flex-end`: Pushes all the items to the end of the container.  
- `center`: Centers all the items in the container.  
- `space-between`: Distributes the items evenly, with the first item at the very beginning and the last item at the very end. The space is placed between the items.  
- `space-around`: Distributes the items with equal space around each item.  
- `space-evenly`: Distributes the items so that the space between each item is exactly the same, and the space at the ends is also the same.

---
**align-items**\
This property controls how items are aligned along the **cross axis**. The cross axis is perpendicular to the main axis (by default, this is top to bottom).

- `flex-start`: Pushes all the items to the top of the container.  
- `flex-end`: Pushes all the items to the bottom of the container.  
- `center`: Centers all the items vertically in the container.  
- `baseline`: Aligns items based on their text content's baseline.  
- `stretch`: Stretches the items to fill the entire height of the container.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
